title: LaGrande Technology — A Proposal: Consumer Market Research for Intel LT
author: Peter S. Kastner
methodology: Proposal document; qualitative consumer/business research; surveys; focus groups; IDIs
abstract: Letter and attached proposal from Peter S. Kastner (Aberdeen Group EVP Research) to Michael Ferron-Jones (Intel Desktop Platform Analyst Relations) dated October 5, 2003. Proposes a multi-phase market research program to evaluate consumer LaGrande Technology (LT) — Intel's hardware-based trusted computing platform. Aberdeen argues Intel is under-valuing the consumer LT opportunity, estimating >$150M annual revenue opportunity and 10M+ consumer units in 2006 at $100 retail uplift. Proposes 3-phase research (consumer qualitative, quantitative tracking, localized surveys) in US, Canada, Japan, Germany, UK, France, Italy, Brazil, Mexico. Research focuses on identity theft fear, privacy, trusted computing adoption drivers and barriers.
importance_rationale: Rare primary source documenting Aberdeen's commercial research pitch to Intel on LaGrande/TXT trusted computing, a foundational security technology. Reveals 2003 market sizing assumptions and strategic disagreement with Intel's business-only focus.
relevance_rationale: Directly documents the pre-launch consumer trusted computing debate for what became Intel TXT/TPM ecosystem; captures Kastner's market intelligence methodology.
prescience_rationale: LT/TXT was adopted primarily in enterprise (as Intel planned), with limited consumer uptake; consumer privacy concerns proved prescient but monetization via hardware uplift did not materialize as predicted.
